text: In the last chapter, we dealt with equations, inequalities, and basic mathematical statements like “ \(x\) divides \(y\) .” Complex mathematical statements are built up from simple ones like these using logical terms like “and,” “or,” “not,” and “if … then,” “every,” and “some.” In this chapter, we show you how to work with statements that are built up in this way.
html: In the last chapter, we dealt with equations, inequalities, and basic mathematical statements like “\(x\) divides \(y\).” Complex mathematical statements are built up from simple ones like these using logical terms like “and,” “or,” “not,” and “if … then,” “every,” and “some.” In this chapter, we show you how to work with statements that are built up in this way.
zh: 上一章中，我们处理了等式、不等式，以及诸如“\(x\) 整除 \(y\)”这样的基本数学命题。复杂的数学命题由这类简单命题借助逻辑术语构成，例如“且”“或”“非”，以及“若……则……”“每个”和“存在某个”。本章将说明如何处理以这种方式构成的命题。

text: In words, we would say “for every real number x , if 0 ≤ x then the absolute value of x equals x ”. We can also have more complicated statements like:

zh: 用文字来说，我们会说：“对每个实数 x，若 0 ≤ x，则 x 的绝对值等于 x。”我们也可以有更复杂的命题，例如：

text: In words, we would say “for every x , y , and ε , if 0 < ε ≤ 1 , the absolute value of x is less than ε , and the absolute value of y is less than ε , then the absolute value of x * y is less than ε .” In Lean, in a sequence of implications there are implicit parentheses grouped to the right. So the expression above means “if 0 < ε then if ε ≤ 1 then if |x| < ε …” As a result, the expression says that all the assumptions together imply the conclusion.
html: In words, we would say “for every x, y, and ε, if 0 < ε ≤ 1, the absolute value of x is less than ε, and the absolute value of y is less than ε, then the absolute value of x * y is less than ε.” In Lean, in a sequence of implications there are implicit parentheses grouped to the right. So the expression above means “if 0 < ε then if ε ≤ 1 then if |x| < ε …” As a result, the expression says that all the assumptions together imply the conclusion.
zh: 用文字来说，我们会说：“对每个 x、y 和 ε，若 0 < ε ≤ 1，x 的绝对值小于 ε，且 y 的绝对值小于 ε，则 x * y 的绝对值小于 ε。”在 Lean 中，一串蕴含中的隐含括号向右结合。因此，上面的表达式意为：“若 0 < ε，则若 ε ≤ 1，则若 |x| < ε，……”于是，该表达式表示所有假设合在一起推出结论。

text: You have also already seen that it is common in Lean to use curly brackets to make quantified variables implicit when they can be inferred from subsequent hypotheses. When we do that, we can just apply a lemma to the hypotheses without mentioning the objects.

text: At this stage, you also know that if you use the apply tactic to apply my_lemma to a goal of the form |a * b| < δ , you are left with new goals that require you to prove each of the hypotheses.

text: Universal quantifiers are often hidden in definitions, and Lean will unfold definitions to expose them when necessary. For example, let’s define two predicates, FnUb f a and FnLb f a , where f is a function from the real numbers to the real numbers and a is a real number. The first says that a is an upper bound on the values of f , and the second says that a is a lower bound on the values of f .
html: Universal quantifiers are often hidden in definitions, and Lean will unfold definitions to expose them when necessary. For example, let’s define two predicates, FnUb f a and FnLb f a, where f is a function from the real numbers to the real numbers and a is a real number. The first says that a is an upper bound on the values of f, and the second says that a is a lower bound on the values of f.
zh: 全称量词常常隐藏在定义之中；必要时，Lean 会展开定义以显露它们。例如，让我们定义两个谓词 FnUb f a 和 FnLb f a，其中 f 是从实数到实数的函数，a 是实数。前者表示 a 是 f 的取值的上界，后者表示 a 是 f 的取值的下界。

text: In the next example, fun x ↦ f x + g x is the function that maps x to f x + g x . Going from the expression f x + g x to this function is called a lambda abstraction in type theory.
html: In the next example, fun x ↦ f x + g x is the function that maps x to f x + g x. Going from the expression f x + g x to this function is called a lambda abstraction in type theory.
zh: 在下一个例子中，fun x ↦ f x + g x 是把 x 映到 f x + g x 的函数。在类型论中，从表达式 f x + g x 到这个函数的过程称为 lambda 抽象。

text: Applying intro to the goal FnUb (fun x ↦ f x + g x) (a + b) forces Lean to unfold the definition of FnUb and introduce x for the universal quantifier. The goal is then (fun (x : ℝ) ↦ f x + g x) x ≤ a + b . But applying (fun x ↦ f x + g x) to x should result in f x + g x , and the dsimp command performs that simplification. (The “d” stands for “definitional.”) You can delete that command and the proof still works; Lean would have to perform that contraction anyhow to make sense of the next apply . The dsimp command simply makes the goal more readable and helps us figure out what to do next. Another option is to use the change tactic by writing change f x + g x ≤ a + b . This helps make the proof more readable, and gives you more control over how the goal is transformed.
html: Applying intro to the goal FnUb (fun x ↦ f x + g x) (a + b) forces Lean to unfold the definition of FnUb and introduce x for the universal quantifier. The goal is then (fun (x : ℝ) ↦ f x + g x) x ≤ a + b. But applying (fun x ↦ f x + g x) to x should result in f x + g x, and the dsimp command performs that simplification. (The “d” stands for “definitional.”) You can delete that command and the proof still works; Lean would have to perform that contraction anyhow to make sense of the next apply. The dsimp command simply makes the goal more readable and helps us figure out what to do next. Another option is to use the change tactic by writing change f x + g x ≤ a + b. This helps make the proof more readable, and gives you more control over how the goal is transformed.
zh: 对目标 FnUb (fun x ↦ f x + g x) (a + b) 应用 intro，会迫使 Lean 展开 FnUb 的定义，并为全称量词引入 x。此时目标变为 (fun (x : ℝ) ↦ f x + g x) x ≤ a + b。但是，把 (fun x ↦ f x + g x) 应用于 x 应得到 f x + g x，而 dsimp 命令执行这一化简。（其中 “d” 表示 “definitional”。）删除该命令后证明仍然成立；Lean 无论如何都必须执行这个约简，才能理解下一个 apply。dsimp 只是让目标更可读，并帮助我们判断下一步该做什么。另一种选择是使用 change 策略，写成 change f x + g x ≤ a + b。这有助于提高证明的可读性，并使你能更好地控制目标如何变形。

text: The rest of the proof is routine. The last two apply commands force Lean to unfold the definitions of FnUb in the hypotheses. Try carrying out similar proofs of these:
html: The rest of the proof is routine. The last two apply commands force Lean to unfold the definitions of FnUb in the hypotheses. Try carrying out similar proofs of these:
zh: 证明的其余部分是例行的。最后两个 apply 命令迫使 Lean 展开假设中 FnUb 的定义。请尝试以类似方式完成下面这些证明：

text: Even though we have defined FnUb and FnLb for functions from the reals to the reals, you should recognize that the definitions and proofs are much more general. The definitions make sense for functions between any two types for which there is a notion of order on the codomain. Checking the type of the theorem add_le_add shows that it holds of any monoid structure that respects the relevant order; the details of what that means don’t matter now, but it is worth knowing that the natural numbers, integers, rationals, and real numbers are all instances. So if we prove the theorem fnUb_add at that level of generality, it will apply in all these instances.
html: Even though we have defined FnUb and FnLb for functions from the reals to the reals, you should recognize that the definitions and proofs are much more general. The definitions make sense for functions between any two types for which there is a notion of order on the codomain. Checking the type of the theorem add_le_add shows that it holds of any monoid structure that respects the relevant order; the details of what that means don’t matter now, but it is worth knowing that the natural numbers, integers, rationals, and real numbers are all instances. So if we prove the theorem fnUb_add at that level of generality, it will apply in all these instances.
zh: 尽管我们为从实数到实数的函数定义了 FnUb 和 FnLb，但你应当认识到，这些定义和证明要一般得多。只要函数的陪域带有某种序结构，这些定义就适用于任意两个类型之间的函数。检查定理 add_le_add 的类型会发现，它适用于任何与相关序相容的幺半群结构；这究竟是什么意思目前并不重要，但值得知道自然数、整数、有理数和实数都是其实例。因此，如果我们在这个一般性层次证明定理 fnUb_add，它就可用于所有这些实例。

text: You have already seen square brackets like these in Section Section 2.2 , though we still haven’t explained what they mean. For concreteness, we will stick to the real numbers for most of our examples, but it is worth knowing that Mathlib contains definitions and theorems that work at a high level of generality.
html: You have already seen square brackets like these in Section Section 2.2, though we still haven’t explained what they mean. For concreteness, we will stick to the real numbers for most of our examples, but it is worth knowing that Mathlib contains definitions and theorems that work at a high level of generality.
zh: 你已经在 第 2.2 节 中见过这样的方括号，尽管我们尚未解释它们的含义。为了具体起见，本书多数例子会坚持使用实数；但值得知道的是，Mathlib 包含许多在高度一般性下工作的定义和定理。

text: For another example of a hidden universal quantifier, Mathlib defines a predicate Monotone , which says that a function is nondecreasing in its arguments:
html: For another example of a hidden universal quantifier, Mathlib defines a predicate Monotone, which says that a function is nondecreasing in its arguments:
zh: 作为隐藏全称量词的另一个例子，Mathlib 定义了谓词 Monotone，表示函数关于其参数单调不减：

text: The property Monotone f is defined to be exactly the expression after the colon. We need to put the @ symbol before h because if we don’t, Lean expands the implicit arguments to h and inserts placeholders.
html: The property Monotone f is defined to be exactly the expression after the colon. We need to put the @ symbol before h because if we don’t, Lean expands the implicit arguments to h and inserts placeholders.
zh: 性质 Monotone f 按定义恰好就是冒号之后的表达式。我们需要在 h 前写符号 @；否则 Lean 会展开 h 的隐式参数并插入占位符。

text: Proving statements about monotonicity involves using intro to introduce two variables, say, a and b , and the hypothesis a ≤ b . To use a monotonicity hypothesis, you can apply it to suitable arguments and hypotheses, and then apply the resulting expression to the goal. Or you can apply it to the goal and let Lean help you work backwards by displaying the remaining hypotheses as new subgoals.
html: Proving statements about monotonicity involves using intro to introduce two variables, say, a and b, and the hypothesis a ≤ b. To use a monotonicity hypothesis, you can apply it to suitable arguments and hypotheses, and then apply the resulting expression to the goal. Or you can apply it to the goal and let Lean help you work backwards by displaying the remaining hypotheses as new subgoals.
zh: 证明关于单调性的命题时，通常使用 intro 引入两个变量，例如 a 和 b，以及假设 a ≤ b。要使用单调性假设，可以把它应用于合适的参数和假设，然后把得到的表达式应用于目标。也可以把它应用于目标，让 Lean 通过把剩余假设显示为新的子目标来帮助你反向推理。

text: When a proof is this short, it is often convenient to give a proof term instead. To describe a proof that temporarily introduces objects a and b and a hypothesis aleb , Lean uses the notation fun a b aleb ↦ ... . This is analogous to the way that an expression like fun x ↦ x^2 describes a function by temporarily naming an object, x , and then using it to describe a value. So the intro command in the previous proof corresponds to the lambda abstraction in the next proof term. The apply commands then correspond to building the application of the theorem to its arguments.
html: When a proof is this short, it is often convenient to give a proof term instead. To describe a proof that temporarily introduces objects a and b and a hypothesis aleb, Lean uses the notation fun a b aleb ↦ .... This is analogous to the way that an expression like fun x ↦ x^2 describes a function by temporarily naming an object, x, and then using it to describe a value. So the intro command in the previous proof corresponds to the lambda abstraction in the next proof term. The apply commands then correspond to building the application of the theorem to its arguments.
zh: 当证明如此简短时，直接给出证明项往往很方便。为了描述一个临时引入对象 a、b 和假设 aleb 的证明，Lean 使用记号 fun a b aleb ↦ ...。这类似于表达式 fun x ↦ x^2 通过临时命名一个对象 x，然后用它描述一个值来定义函数。因此，前一个证明中的 intro 命令对应下一个证明项中的 lambda 抽象，而 apply 命令则对应构造定理对其参数的应用。

text: Here is a useful trick: if you start writing the proof term fun a b aleb ↦ _ using an underscore where the rest of the expression should go, Lean will flag an error, indicating that it can’t guess the value of that expression. If you check the Lean InfoView window in VS Code or hover over the squiggly error marker, Lean will show you the goal that the remaining expression has to solve.
html: Here is a useful trick: if you start writing the proof term fun a b aleb ↦ _ using an underscore where the rest of the expression should go, Lean will flag an error, indicating that it can’t guess the value of that expression. If you check the Lean InfoView window in VS Code or hover over the squiggly error marker, Lean will show you the goal that the remaining expression has to solve.
zh: 这里有一个有用技巧：如果你开始写证明项 fun a b aleb ↦ _，用下划线表示剩余表达式应出现的位置，Lean 会标出错误，说明它无法猜出该表达式的值。若在 VS Code 中查看 Lean InfoView 窗口，或把鼠标悬停在波浪线错误标记上，Lean 会显示剩余表达式必须解决的目标。

text: You’ll notice Lean prints warnings about the above proof, saying that a and b are not used in the body of the function. This is a general mechanism that is useful for instance to avoid having unnecessary assumptions in a theorem. Here it is not really telling us anything useful. We can either disable this warning until the end of the current section by writing set_option linter.unusedVariables false or only in the example by writing set_option linter.unusedVariables false in above the example.
html: You’ll notice Lean prints warnings about the above proof, saying that a and b are not used in the body of the function. This is a general mechanism that is useful for instance to avoid having unnecessary assumptions in a theorem. Here it is not really telling us anything useful. We can either disable this warning until the end of the current section by writing set_option linter.unusedVariables false or only in the example by writing set_option linter.unusedVariables false in above the example.
zh: 你会注意到，Lean 会对上述证明打印警告，说 <cite>a</cite> 和 <cite>b</cite> 未在函数体中使用。这是一种通用机制，例如可用于避免定理中含有不必要的假设。这里它并没有告诉我们什么真正有用的信息。我们可以写 <cite>set_option linter.unusedVariables false</cite>，将这个警告禁用到当前小节结束；也可以只在该例子中禁用，在例子上方写 <cite>set_option linter.unusedVariables false in</cite>。

text: We can also replace a and b by underscores to tell Lean we don’t want to name them. Lean will then use auto-generated inaccessible names such as x✝ . Alternatively we can prefix our names with underscores to tell Lean we do not intend to use them.
html: We can also replace a and b by underscores to tell Lean we don’t want to name them. Lean will then use auto-generated inaccessible names such as x✝. Alternatively we can prefix our names with underscores to tell Lean we do not intend to use them.
zh: 我们也可以把 <cite>a</cite> 和 <cite>b</cite> 替换为下划线，告诉 Lean 不想给它们命名。Lean 随后会使用自动生成的不可访问名称，例如 <cite>x✝</cite>。另一种选择是在名称前加下划线，告诉 Lean 我们并不打算使用它们。

text: Here are some more examples. A function \(f\) from \(\Bbb R\) to \(\Bbb R\) is said to be even if \(f(-x) = f(x)\) for every \(x\) , and odd if \(f(-x) = -f(x)\) for every \(x\) . The following example defines these two notions formally and establishes one fact about them. You can complete the proofs of the others.
html: Here are some more examples. A function \(f\) from \(\Bbb R\) to \(\Bbb R\) is said to be even if \(f(-x) = f(x)\) for every \(x\), and odd if \(f(-x) = -f(x)\) for every \(x\). The following example defines these two notions formally and establishes one fact about them. You can complete the proofs of the others.
zh: 下面还有一些例子。从 \(\Bbb R\) 到 \(\Bbb R\) 的函数 \(f\)，若对每个 \(x\) 都有 \(f(-x) = f(x)\)，则称为偶函数；若对每个 \(x\) 都有 \(f(-x) = -f(x)\)，则称为奇函数。下面的例子形式化定义这两个概念，并证明其中一个事实。你可以完成其余证明。

text: The first proof can be shortened using dsimp or change to get rid of the lambda abstraction. But you can check that the subsequent rw won’t work unless we get rid of the lambda abstraction explicitly, because otherwise it cannot find the patterns f x and g x in the expression. Contrary to some other tactics, rw operates on the syntactic level, it won’t unfold definitions or apply reductions for you (it has a variant called erw that tries a little harder in this direction, but not much harder).
html: The first proof can be shortened using dsimp or change to get rid of the lambda abstraction. But you can check that the subsequent rw won’t work unless we get rid of the lambda abstraction explicitly, because otherwise it cannot find the patterns f x and g x in the expression. Contrary to some other tactics, rw operates on the syntactic level, it won’t unfold definitions or apply reductions for you (it has a variant called erw that tries a little harder in this direction, but not much harder).
zh: 第一个证明可以用 dsimp 或 change 缩短，以消除 lambda 抽象。但你可以检查，如果不显式消除 lambda 抽象，后续 rw 将无法工作，因为它无法在表达式中找到模式 f x 和 g x。与某些其他策略不同，rw 在句法层面操作；它不会替你展开定义或进行约简（它有一个变体 erw，会在这个方向上稍微多尝试一点，但也不会多很多）。

text: You can find implicit universal quantifiers all over the place, once you know how to spot them.
html: You can find implicit universal quantifiers all over the place, once you know how to spot them.
zh: 一旦知道如何识别隐式全称量词，就会发现它们无处不在。

text: Mathlib includes a good library for manipulating sets. Recall that Lean does not use foundations based on set theory, so here the word set has its mundane meaning of a collection of mathematical objects of some given type α . If x has type α and s has type Set α , then x ∈ s is a proposition that asserts that x is an element of s . If y has some different type β then the expression y ∈ s makes no sense. Here “makes no sense” means “has no type hence Lean does not accept it as a well-formed statement”. This contrasts with Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory for instance where a ∈ b is a well-formed statement for both mathematical objects a and b . For instance sin ∈ cos is a well-formed statement in ZF. This defect of set theoretic foundations is an important motivation for not using it in a proof assistant which is meant to assist us by detecting meaningless expressions. In Lean sin has type ℝ → ℝ and cos has type ℝ → ℝ which is not equal to Set (ℝ → ℝ) , even after unfolding definitions, so the statement sin ∈ cos makes no sense. One can also use Lean to work on set theory itself. For instance the independence of the continuum hypothesis from the axioms of Zermelo-Fraenkel has been formalized in Lean. But such a meta-theory of set theory is completely beyond the scope of this book.

Here “makes no sense” means “has no type hence Lean does not accept it as a well-formed statement”. One can also use Lean to work on set theory itself. For instance the independence of the continuum hypothesis from the axioms of Zermelo-Fraenkel has been formalized in Lean. But such a meta-theory of set theory is completely beyond the scope of this book.
zh: Mathlib 包含一个很好的集合操作库。回忆一下，Lean 并不使用基于集合论的基础，因此这里“集合”一词具有日常数学意义：给定某个类型 α 的数学对象所组成的一个汇集。若 x 的类型是 α，而 s 的类型是 Set α，则 x ∈ s 是一个命题，断言 x 是 s 的元素。若 y 具有某个不同类型 β，则表达式 y ∈ s 没有意义。这里“没有意义”指的是“没有类型，因而 Lean 不接受它作为良构命题”。这与 Zermelo-Fraenkel 集合论形成对比；在后者中，对任意数学对象 a 和 b，a ∈ b 都是良构命题。例如，sin ∈ cos 在 ZF 中是良构命题。集合论基础的这一缺陷，是证明助手不采用它的重要动机之一：证明助手应通过发现无意义表达式来帮助我们。在 Lean 中，sin 的类型是 ℝ → ℝ，cos 的类型也是 ℝ → ℝ，即使展开定义后也不等于 Set (ℝ → ℝ)，所以命题 sin ∈ cos 没有意义。人们也可以用 Lean 研究集合论本身。例如，连续统假设相对于 Zermelo-Fraenkel 公理的独立性已经在 Lean 中形式化。但这种集合论元理论完全超出了本书范围。

text: If s and t are of type Set α , then the subset relation s ⊆ t is defined to mean ∀ {x : α}, x ∈ s → x ∈ t . The variable in the quantifier is marked implicit so that given h : s ⊆ t and h' : x ∈ s , we can write h h' as justification for x ∈ t . The following example provides a tactic proof and a proof term justifying the reflexivity of the subset relation, and asks you to do the same for transitivity.
html: If s and t are of type Set α, then the subset relation s ⊆ t is defined to mean ∀ {x : α}, x ∈ s → x ∈ t. The variable in the quantifier is marked implicit so that given h : s ⊆ t and h' : x ∈ s, we can write h h' as justification for x ∈ t. The following example provides a tactic proof and a proof term justifying the reflexivity of the subset relation, and asks you to do the same for transitivity.
zh: 若 s 和 t 的类型是 Set α，则子集关系 s ⊆ t 定义为 ∀ {x : α}, x ∈ s → x ∈ t。量词中的变量被标记为隐式变量，因此给定 h : s ⊆ t 和 h' : x ∈ s 时，可以写 h h' 作为 x ∈ t 的证明。下面的例子给出了子集关系自反性的策略证明和证明项，并要求你对传递性做同样的事情。

text: Just as we defined FnUb for functions, we can define SetUb s a to mean that a is an upper bound on the set s , assuming s is a set of elements of some type that has an order associated with it. In the next example, we ask you to prove that if a is a bound on s and a ≤ b , then b is a bound on s as well.
html: Just as we defined FnUb for functions, we can define SetUb s a to mean that a is an upper bound on the set s, assuming s is a set of elements of some type that has an order associated with it. In the next example, we ask you to prove that if a is a bound on s and a ≤ b, then b is a bound on s as well.
zh: 正如我们为函数定义 FnUb 一样，也可以定义 SetUb s a，表示 a 是集合 s 的上界；这里假定 s 是某个带序类型中元素组成的集合。在下一个例子中，我们要求你证明：如果 a 是 s 的上界且 a ≤ b，那么 b 也是 s 的上界。

text: We close this section with one last important example. A function \(f\) is said to be injective if for every \(x_1\) and \(x_2\) , if \(f(x_1) = f(x_2)\) then \(x_1 = x_2\) . Mathlib defines Function.Injective f with x₁ and x₂ implicit. The next example shows that, on the real numbers, any function that adds a constant is injective. We then ask you to show that multiplication by a nonzero constant is also injective, using the lemma name in the example as a source of inspiration. Recall you should use Ctrl-space completion after guessing the beginning of a lemma name.

Mathlib defines Function.Injective f with x₁ and x₂ implicit. The next example shows that, on the real numbers, any function that adds a constant is injective. We then ask you to show that multiplication by a nonzero constant is also injective, using the lemma name in the example as a source of inspiration. Recall you should use Ctrl-space completion after guessing the beginning of a lemma name.
zh: 本节最后看一个重要例子。若对每个 \(x_1\) 和 \(x_2\)，由 \(f(x_1) = f(x_2)\) 可推出 \(x_1 = x_2\)，则称函数 \(f\) 是单射。Mathlib 定义了 Function.Injective f，其中 x₁ 和 x₂ 为隐式参数。下一个例子说明，在实数上，任何加上常数的函数都是单射。随后我们要求你证明，乘以非零常数的函数也是单射；可以从例子中的引理名获得启发。记住，在猜出引理名开头之后，应使用 Ctrl-space 补全。

text: The existential quantifier, which can be entered as \ex in VS Code, is used to represent the phrase “there exists.” The formal expression ∃ x : ℝ, 2 < x ∧ x < 3 in Lean says that there is a real number between 2 and 3. (We will discuss the conjunction symbol, ∧ , in Section 3.4 .) The canonical way to prove such a statement is to exhibit a real number and show that it has the stated property. The number 2.5, which we can enter as 5 / 2 or (5 : ℝ) / 2 when Lean cannot infer from context that we have the real numbers in mind, has the required property, and the norm_num tactic can prove that it meets the description.
html: The existential quantifier, which can be entered as \ex in VS Code, is used to represent the phrase “there exists.” The formal expression ∃ x : ℝ, 2 < x ∧ x < 3 in Lean says that there is a real number between 2 and 3. (We will discuss the conjunction symbol, ∧, in Section 3.4.) The canonical way to prove such a statement is to exhibit a real number and show that it has the stated property. The number 2.5, which we can enter as 5 / 2 or (5 : ℝ) / 2 when Lean cannot infer from context that we have the real numbers in mind, has the required property, and the norm_num tactic can prove that it meets the description.
zh: 存在量词可以在 VS Code 中输入为 \ex，用于表示“存在”。Lean 中的形式表达式 ∃ x : ℝ, 2 < x ∧ x < 3 表示在 2 与 3 之间存在一个实数。（我们将在 第 3.4 节 中讨论合取符号 ∧。）证明这类命题的标准方式，是给出一个实数，并证明它具有所陈述的性质。数 2.5 具有所需性质；它可以输入为 5 / 2，或在 Lean 无法从语境推断我们指的是实数时输入为 (5 : ℝ) / 2；norm_num 策略可以证明它满足该描述。

text: There are a few ways we can put the information together. Given a goal that begins with an existential quantifier, the use tactic is used to provide the object, leaving the goal of proving the property.
html: There are a few ways we can put the information together. Given a goal that begins with an existential quantifier, the use tactic is used to provide the object, leaving the goal of proving the property.
zh: 有几种方式可以把这些信息组合起来。若目标以存在量词开头，use 策略用于给出对象，并留下证明该对象满足相应性质的目标。

zh: 事实上，use 策略还会自动尝试使用可用假设。

text: Alternatively, we can use Lean’s anonymous constructor notation to construct a proof of an existential quantifier.
html: Alternatively, we can use Lean’s anonymous constructor notation to construct a proof of an existential quantifier.
zh: 另一种方式是使用 Lean 的匿名构造子记号来构造存在量词的证明。

The left and right angle brackets, which can be entered as \< and \> respectively, tell Lean to put together the given data using whatever construction is appropriate for the current goal. text: So now we know how to prove an exists statement. But how do we use one? If we know that there exists an object with a certain property, we should be able to give a name to an arbitrary one and reason about it. For example, remember the predicates FnUb f a and FnLb f a from the last section, which say that a is an upper bound or lower bound on f , respectively. We can use the existential quantifier to say that “ f is bounded” without specifying the bound:
html: So now we know how to prove an exists statement. But how do we use one? If we know that there exists an object with a certain property, we should be able to give a name to an arbitrary one and reason about it. For example, remember the predicates FnUb f a and FnLb f a from the last section, which say that a is an upper bound or lower bound on f, respectively. We can use the existential quantifier to say that “f is bounded” without specifying the bound:
zh: 现在我们知道如何证明存在命题。那么如何使用存在命题呢？如果已知存在一个具有某性质的对象，就应当能够给其中任意一个命名并对它推理。例如，回忆上一节中的谓词 FnUb f a 和 FnLb f a，它们分别表示 a 是 f 的上界或下界。我们可以用存在量词说“f 有界”，而不指明具体界：

text: We can use the theorem FnUb_add from the last section to prove that if f and g have upper bounds, then so does fun x ↦ f x + g x .
html: We can use the theorem FnUb_add from the last section to prove that if f and g have upper bounds, then so does fun x ↦ f x + g x.
zh: 我们可以使用上一节中的定理 FnUb_add 证明：若 f 和 g 都有上界，则 fun x ↦ f x + g x 也有上界。

text: The rcases tactic unpacks the information in the existential quantifier. The annotations like ⟨a, ubfa⟩ , written with the same angle brackets as the anonymous constructors, are known as patterns , and they describe the information that we expect to find when we unpack the main argument. Given the hypothesis ubf that there is an upper bound for f , rcases ubf with ⟨a, ubfa⟩ adds a new variable a for an upper bound to the context, together with the hypothesis ubfa that it has the given property. The goal is left unchanged; what has changed is that we can now use the new object and the new hypothesis to prove the goal. This is a common method of reasoning in mathematics: we unpack objects whose existence is asserted or implied by some hypothesis, and then use it to establish the existence of something else.
html: The rcases tactic unpacks the information in the existential quantifier. The annotations like ⟨a, ubfa⟩, written with the same angle brackets as the anonymous constructors, are known as patterns, and they describe the information that we expect to find when we unpack the main argument. Given the hypothesis ubf that there is an upper bound for f, rcases ubf with ⟨a, ubfa⟩ adds a new variable a for an upper bound to the context, together with the hypothesis ubfa that it has the given property. The goal is left unchanged; what has changed is that we can now use the new object and the new hypothesis to prove the goal. This is a common method of reasoning in mathematics: we unpack objects whose existence is asserted or implied by some hypothesis, and then use it to establish the existence of something else.
zh: rcases 策略会拆开存在量词中的信息。像 ⟨a, ubfa⟩ 这样的标注使用与匿名构造子相同的尖括号，称为模式；它描述了我们在拆开主参数时预期得到的信息。给定假设 ubf，表示 f 有上界，命令 rcases ubf with ⟨a, ubfa⟩ 会向语境加入一个新变量 a，表示某个上界，同时加入假设 ubfa，表示它具有给定性质。目标保持不变；改变的是我们现在可以使用这个新对象和新假设来证明目标。这是数学推理中的常见方法：我们拆开由某个假设断言或蕴含其存在性的对象，然后用它建立其他事物的存在性。

text: Try using this method to establish the following. You might find it useful to turn some of the examples from the last section into named theorems, as we did with fn_ub_add , or you can insert the arguments directly into the proofs.
html: Try using this method to establish the following. You might find it useful to turn some of the examples from the last section into named theorems, as we did with fn_ub_add, or you can insert the arguments directly into the proofs.

text: In the first example, if you put your cursor after cases ubf , you will see that the tactic produces a single goal, which Lean has tagged intro . (The particular name chosen comes from the internal name for the axiomatic primitive that builds a proof of an existential statement.) The case tactic then names the components. The second example is similar, except using next instead of case means that you can avoid mentioning intro . The word match in the last two examples highlights that what we are doing here is what computer scientists call “pattern matching.” Notice that the third proof begins by by , after which the tactic version of match expects a tactic proof on the right side of the arrow. The last example is a proof term: there are no tactics in sight.
html: In the first example, if you put your cursor after cases ubf, you will see that the tactic produces a single goal, which Lean has tagged intro. (The particular name chosen comes from the internal name for the axiomatic primitive that builds a proof of an existential statement.) The case tactic then names the components. The second example is similar, except using next instead of case means that you can avoid mentioning intro. The word match in the last two examples highlights that what we are doing here is what computer scientists call “pattern matching.” Notice that the third proof begins by by, after which the tactic version of match expects a tactic proof on the right side of the arrow. The last example is a proof term: there are no tactics in sight.
zh: 在第一个例子中，如果把光标放在 cases ubf 之后，会看到该策略产生一个目标，Lean 给它标记为 intro。（这个具体名称来自用于构造存在命题证明的公理化原语的内部名称。）随后 case 策略为各组成部分命名。第二个例子类似，只是使用 next 而非 case，从而可以避免提及 intro。最后两个例子中的 match 一词强调了这里所做的正是计算机科学家所谓的“模式匹配”。注意，第三个证明以 by 开始；在它之后，策略版本的 match 要求箭头右侧是一个策略证明。最后一个例子是证明项：完全没有策略出现。

text: For the rest of this book, we will stick to rcases , rintro , and obtain , as the preferred ways of using an existential quantifier. But it can’t hurt to see the alternative syntax, especially if there is a chance you will find yourself in the company of computer scientists.
html: For the rest of this book, we will stick to rcases, rintro, and obtain, as the preferred ways of using an existential quantifier. But it can’t hurt to see the alternative syntax, especially if there is a chance you will find yourself in the company of computer scientists.
zh: 在本书余下部分，我们会坚持使用 rcases、rintro 和 obtain，作为使用存在量词的首选方式。不过，了解替代语法并无坏处，尤其是在你可能与计算机科学家共处时。

text: To illustrate one way that rcases can be used, we prove an old mathematical chestnut: if two integers x and y can each be written as a sum of two squares, then so can their product, x * y . In fact, the statement is true for any commutative ring, not just the integers. In the next example, rcases unpacks two existential quantifiers at once. We then provide the magic values needed to express x * y as a sum of squares as a list to the use statement, and we use ring to verify that they work.
html: To illustrate one way that rcases can be used, we prove an old mathematical chestnut: if two integers x and y can each be written as a sum of two squares, then so can their product, x * y. In fact, the statement is true for any commutative ring, not just the integers. In the next example, rcases unpacks two existential quantifiers at once. We then provide the magic values needed to express x * y as a sum of squares as a list to the use statement, and we use ring to verify that they work.
zh: 为了说明 rcases 的一种用法，我们证明一个古老的数学常识：若两个整数 x 和 y 都可以写成两个平方数之和，则它们的乘积 x * y 也可以写成两个平方数之和。事实上，这个命题对任意交换环都成立，而不仅限于整数。在下一个例子中，rcases 一次拆开两个存在量词。然后我们把把 x * y 表示为平方和所需的神奇取值作为列表提供给 use 语句，并用 ring 验证它们有效。

text: This proof doesn’t provide much insight, but here is one way to motivate it. A Gaussian integer is a number of the form \(a + bi\) where \(a\) and \(b\) are integers and \(i = \sqrt{-1}\) . The norm of the Gaussian integer \(a + bi\) is, by definition, \(a^2 + b^2\) . So the norm of a Gaussian integer is a sum of squares, and any sum of squares can be expressed in this way. The theorem above reflects the fact that norm of a product of Gaussian integers is the product of their norms: if \(x\) is the norm of \(a + bi\) and \(y\) in the norm of \(c + di\) , then \(xy\) is the norm of \((a + bi) (c + di)\) . Our cryptic proof illustrates the fact that the proof that is easiest to formalize isn’t always the most perspicuous one. In Section 7.3 , we will provide you with the means to define the Gaussian integers and use them to provide an alternative proof.
html: This proof doesn’t provide much insight, but here is one way to motivate it. A Gaussian integer is a number of the form \(a + bi\) where \(a\) and \(b\) are integers and \(i = \sqrt{-1}\). The norm of the Gaussian integer \(a + bi\) is, by definition, \(a^2 + b^2\). So the norm of a Gaussian integer is a sum of squares, and any sum of squares can be expressed in this way. The theorem above reflects the fact that norm of a product of Gaussian integers is the product of their norms: if \(x\) is the norm of \(a + bi\) and \(y\) in the norm of \(c + di\), then \(xy\) is the norm of \((a + bi) (c + di)\). Our cryptic proof illustrates the fact that the proof that is easiest to formalize isn’t always the most perspicuous one. In Section 7.3, we will provide you with the means to define the Gaussian integers and use them to provide an alternative proof.
zh: 这个证明没有提供太多洞见，但可以用以下方式来说明它的动机。高斯整数是形如 \(a + bi\) 的数，其中 \(a\) 和 \(b\) 是整数，且 \(i = \sqrt{-1}\)。按定义，高斯整数 \(a + bi\) 的范数是 \(a^2 + b^2\)。因此，高斯整数的范数是平方和，且任意平方和都可以这样表示。上述定理反映了这样一个事实：高斯整数乘积的范数等于范数的乘积；如果 \(x\) 是 \(a + bi\) 的范数，而 \(y\) 是 \(c + di\) 的范数，则 \(xy\) 是 \((a + bi)(c + di)\) 的范数。这个晦涩的证明说明，最容易形式化的证明并不总是最清楚的证明。在 第 7.3 节 中，我们会提供定义高斯整数的手段，并用它们给出一个替代证明。

text: The pattern of unpacking an equation inside an existential quantifier and then using it to rewrite an expression in the goal comes up often, so much so that the rcases tactic provides an abbreviation: if you use the keyword rfl in place of a new identifier, rcases does the rewriting automatically (this trick doesn’t work with pattern-matching lambdas).
html: The pattern of unpacking an equation inside an existential quantifier and then using it to rewrite an expression in the goal comes up often, so much so that the rcases tactic provides an abbreviation: if you use the keyword rfl in place of a new identifier, rcases does the rewriting automatically (this trick doesn’t work with pattern-matching lambdas).
zh: 拆开存在量词内部的等式，再用它重写目标中的表达式，这种模式经常出现；频繁到 rcases 策略为它提供了一个缩写：若使用关键字 rfl 代替新的标识符，rcases 会自动执行重写（这个技巧不适用于模式匹配 lambda）。

text: As with the universal quantifier, you can find existential quantifiers hidden all over if you know how to spot them. For example, divisibility is implicitly an “exists” statement.
html: As with the universal quantifier, you can find existential quantifiers hidden all over if you know how to spot them. For example, divisibility is implicitly an “exists” statement.

text: The symbol ¬ is meant to express negation, so ¬ x < y says that x is not less than y , ¬ x = y (or, equivalently, x ≠ y ) says that x is not equal to y , and ¬ ∃ z, x < z ∧ z < y says that there does not exist a z strictly between x and y . In Lean, the notation ¬ A abbreviates A → False , which you can think of as saying that A implies a contradiction. Practically speaking, this means that you already know something about how to work with negations: you can prove ¬ A by introducing a hypothesis h : A and proving False , and if you have h : ¬ A and h' : A , then applying h to h' yields False .
html: The symbol ¬ is meant to express negation, so ¬ x < y says that x is not less than y, ¬ x = y (or, equivalently, x ≠ y) says that x is not equal to y, and ¬ ∃ z, x < z ∧ z < y says that there does not exist a z strictly between x and y. In Lean, the notation ¬ A abbreviates A → False, which you can think of as saying that A implies a contradiction. Practically speaking, this means that you already know something about how to work with negations: you can prove ¬ A by introducing a hypothesis h : A and proving False, and if you have h : ¬ A and h' : A, then applying h to h' yields False.
zh: 符号 ¬ 用来表示否定，因此 ¬ x < y 表示 x 不小于 y，¬ x = y（或等价地，x ≠ y）表示 x 不等于 y，而 ¬ ∃ z, x < z ∧ z < y 表示不存在严格位于 x 与 y 之间的 z。在 Lean 中，记号 ¬ A 是 A → False 的缩写，可以理解为 A 蕴含矛盾。实际而言，这意味着你已经知道一些处理否定的方法：可以通过引入假设 h : A 并证明 False 来证明 ¬ A；若有 h : ¬ A 和 h' : A，则把 h 应用于 h' 就得到 False。

text: To illustrate, consider the irreflexivity principle lt_irrefl for a strict order, which says that we have ¬ a < a for every a . The asymmetry principle lt_asymm says that we have a < b → ¬ b < a . Let’s show that lt_asymm follows from lt_irrefl .
html: To illustrate, consider the irreflexivity principle lt_irrefl for a strict order, which says that we have ¬ a < a for every a. The asymmetry principle lt_asymm says that we have a < b → ¬ b < a. Let’s show that lt_asymm follows from lt_irrefl.
zh: 为说明这一点，考虑严格序的非自反性原理 lt_irrefl，它说对每个 a 都有 ¬ a < a。不对称性原理 lt_asymm 说 a < b → ¬ b < a。我们来证明 lt_asymm 可由 lt_irrefl 推出。

text: This example introduces a couple of new tricks. First, when you use have without providing a label, Lean uses the name this , providing a convenient way to refer back to it. Because the proof is so short, we provide an explicit proof term. text: It is often tedious to work with compound statements with a negation in front, and it is a common mathematical pattern to replace such statements with equivalent forms in which the negation has been pushed inward. text: We close this section with the principle of ex falso , which says that anything follows from a contradiction. In Lean, this is represented by False.elim , which establishes False → P for any proposition P . This may seem like a strange principle, but it comes up fairly often. We often prove a theorem by splitting on cases, and sometimes we can show that one of the cases is contradictory. In that case, we need to assert that the contradiction establishes the goal so we can move on to the next one. (We will see instances of reasoning by cases in Section 3.5 .)
html: We close this section with the principle of ex falso, which says that anything follows from a contradiction. In Lean, this is represented by False.elim, which establishes False → P for any proposition P. This may seem like a strange principle, but it comes up fairly often. We often prove a theorem by splitting on cases, and sometimes we can show that one of the cases is contradictory. In that case, we need to assert that the contradiction establishes the goal so we can move on to the next one. (We will see instances of reasoning by cases in Section 3.5.)
zh: 本节最后介绍 ex falso 原理，即从矛盾可以推出任何命题。在 Lean 中，这由 False.elim 表示，它对任意命题 P 建立 False → P。这个原理看起来也许奇怪，但经常出现。我们常常通过分情况来证明定理，有时可以证明其中一种情形是矛盾的。在这种情况下，需要断言该矛盾建立了当前目标，以便继续处理下一个情形。（我们将在 第 3.5 节 中看到分情况推理的实例。）

text: Lean provides a number of ways of closing a goal once a contradiction has been reached.

zh: 一旦已经得到矛盾，Lean 提供了若干关闭目标的方法。

text: The exfalso tactic replaces the current goal with the goal of proving False . Given h : P and h' : ¬ P , the term absurd h h' establishes any proposition. Finally, the contradiction tactic tries to close a goal by finding a contradiction in the hypotheses, such as a pair of the form h : P and h' : ¬ P . Of course, in this example, linarith also works.
html: The exfalso tactic replaces the current goal with the goal of proving False. Given h : P and h' : ¬ P, the term absurd h h' establishes any proposition. Finally, the contradiction tactic tries to close a goal by finding a contradiction in the hypotheses, such as a pair of the form h : P and h' : ¬ P. Of course, in this example, linarith also works.
zh: exfalso 策略把当前目标替换为证明 False 的目标。给定 h : P 和 h' : ¬ P，项 absurd h h' 可以建立任意命题。最后，contradiction 策略会尝试通过在假设中寻找矛盾来关闭目标，例如寻找形如 h : P 与 h' : ¬ P 的一对假设。当然，在这个例子中，linarith 也能工作。

You have already seen that you can write h.mp and h.mpr or h.1 and h.2 for the two directions of h : A ↔ B . You can also use cases and friends. To prove an if-and-only-if statement, you can use constructor or angle brackets, just as you would if you were proving a conjunction.
html: In Lean, A ↔ B is not defined to be (A → B) ∧ (B → A), but it could have been, and it behaves roughly the same way. You have already seen that you can write h.mp and h.mpr or h.1 and h.2 for the two directions of h : A ↔ B. You can also use cases and friends. To prove an if-and-only-if statement, you can use constructor or angle brackets, just as you would if you were proving a conjunction.
zh: 在 Lean 中，A ↔ B 并未定义为 (A → B) ∧ (B → A)，但本来可以这样定义，而且它的行为大致相同。你已经看到，对于 h : A ↔ B，可以写 h.mp 和 h.mpr，或 h.1 和 h.2，来获得两个方向。也可以使用 cases 及其相关方法。要证明一个当且仅当命题，可以像证明合取一样使用 constructor 或尖括号。

text: The last proof term is inscrutable. Remember that you can use underscores while writing an expression like that to see what Lean expects.
html: The last proof term is inscrutable. Remember that you can use underscores while writing an expression like that to see what Lean expects.
zh: 最后一个证明项难以读懂。请记住，在写这类表达式时，可以使用下划线查看 Lean 期望什么。

text: Try out the various techniques and gadgets you have just seen in order to prove the following:
html: Try out the various techniques and gadgets you have just seen in order to prove the following:
zh: 请尝试使用刚刚看到的各种技巧和工具证明下面的命题：

text: For a more interesting exercise, show that for any two real numbers x and y , x^2 + y^2 = 0 if and only if x = 0 and y = 0 . We suggest proving an auxiliary lemma using linarith , pow_two_nonneg , and eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero .
html: For a more interesting exercise, show that for any two real numbers x and y, x^2 + y^2 = 0 if and only if x = 0 and y = 0. We suggest proving an auxiliary lemma using linarith, pow_two_nonneg, and eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero.
zh: 作为一个更有趣的练习，请证明：对任意两个实数 x 和 y，x^2 + y^2 = 0 当且仅当 x = 0 且 y = 0。我们建议使用 linarith、pow_two_nonneg 和 eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero 证明一个辅助引理。

text: In Lean, bi-implication leads a double-life. You can treat it like a conjunction and use its two parts separately. But Lean also knows that it is a reflexive, symmetric, and transitive relation between propositions, and you can also use it with calc and rw . It is often convenient to rewrite a statement to an equivalent one. In the next example, we use abs_lt to replace an expression of the form |x| < y by the equivalent expression - y < x ∧ x < y , and in the one after that we use Nat.dvd_gcd_iff to replace an expression of the form m ∣ Nat.gcd n k by the equivalent expression m ∣ n ∧ m ∣ k .
html: In Lean, bi-implication leads a double-life. You can treat it like a conjunction and use its two parts separately. But Lean also knows that it is a reflexive, symmetric, and transitive relation between propositions, and you can also use it with calc and rw. It is often convenient to rewrite a statement to an equivalent one. In the next example, we use abs_lt to replace an expression of the form |x| < y by the equivalent expression - y < x ∧ x < y, and in the one after that we use Nat.dvd_gcd_iff to replace an expression of the form m ∣ Nat.gcd n k by the equivalent expression m ∣ n ∧ m ∣ k.
zh: 在 Lean 中，双向蕴含具有双重身份。可以像合取一样对待它，并分别使用其两个部分。但 Lean 也知道它是命题之间的自反、对称且传递的关系，因此也可以把它与 calc 和 rw 一起使用。把一个命题重写为等价命题常常很方便。在下一个例子中，我们用 abs_lt 把形如 |x| < y 的表达式替换为等价表达式 - y < x ∧ x < y；在再下一个例子中，我们用 Nat.dvd_gcd_iff 把形如 m ∣ Nat.gcd n k 的表达式替换为等价表达式 m ∣ n ∧ m ∣ k。

text: See if you can use rw with the theorem below to provide a short proof that negation is not a nondecreasing function. (Note that push_neg won’t unfold definitions for you, so the rw [Monotone] in the proof of the theorem is needed.)
html: See if you can use rw with the theorem below to provide a short proof that negation is not a nondecreasing function. (Note that push_neg won’t unfold definitions for you, so the rw [Monotone] in the proof of the theorem is needed.)
zh: 看看能否用下面的定理配合 rw 给出一个简短证明，说明取负不是单调不减函数。（注意，push_neg 不会替你展开定义，因此该定理证明中的 rw [Monotone] 是必要的。）

text: The remaining exercises in this section are designed to give you some more practice with conjunction and bi-implication. Remember that a partial order is a binary relation that is transitive, reflexive, and antisymmetric. An even weaker notion sometimes arises: a preorder is just a reflexive, transitive relation. For any pre-order ≤ , Lean axiomatizes the associated strict pre-order by a < b ↔ a ≤ b ∧ ¬ b ≤ a . Show that if ≤ is a partial order, then a < b is equivalent to a ≤ b ∧ a ≠ b :
html: The remaining exercises in this section are designed to give you some more practice with conjunction and bi-implication. Remember that a partial order is a binary relation that is transitive, reflexive, and antisymmetric. An even weaker notion sometimes arises: a preorder is just a reflexive, transitive relation. For any pre-order ≤, Lean axiomatizes the associated strict pre-order by a < b ↔ a ≤ b ∧ ¬ b ≤ a. Show that if ≤ is a partial order, then a < b is equivalent to a ≤ b ∧ a ≠ b:
zh: 本节剩余练习旨在让你进一步练习合取与双向蕴含。记住，偏序是一个满足传递性、自反性和反对称性的二元关系。有时还会出现更弱的概念：预序只是自反且传递的关系。对任意预序 ≤，Lean 通过 a < b ↔ a ≤ b ∧ ¬ b ≤ a 公理化相关的严格预序。请证明，如果 ≤ 是偏序，则 a < b 等价于 a ≤ b ∧ a ≠ b：

text: Beyond logical operations, you do not need anything more than le_refl , le_trans , and le_antisymm . Show that even in the case where ≤ is only assumed to be a preorder, we can prove that the strict order is irreflexive and transitive. In the second example, for convenience, we use the simplifier rather than rw to express < in terms of ≤ and ¬ . We will come back to the simplifier later, but here we are only relying on the fact that it will use the indicated lemma repeatedly, even if it needs to be instantiated to different values.
html: Beyond logical operations, you do not need anything more than le_refl, le_trans, and le_antisymm. Show that even in the case where ≤ is only assumed to be a preorder, we can prove that the strict order is irreflexive and transitive. In the second example, for convenience, we use the simplifier rather than rw to express < in terms of ≤ and ¬. We will come back to the simplifier later, but here we are only relying on the fact that it will use the indicated lemma repeatedly, even if it needs to be instantiated to different values.
zh: 除了逻辑操作之外，你不需要比 le_refl、le_trans 和 le_antisymm 更多的工具。请证明：即使 ≤ 只假定为预序，也能证明严格序是非自反且传递的。在第二个例子中，为方便起见，我们使用化简器而不是 rw，把 < 用 ≤ 和 ¬ 表示出来。我们稍后会回到化简器；这里仅依赖这样一个事实：即使需要把指定引理实例化为不同的值，化简器也会反复使用该引理。

text: The canonical way to prove a disjunction A ∨ B is to prove A or to prove B . The left tactic chooses A , and the right tactic chooses B .
html: The canonical way to prove a disjunction A ∨ B is to prove A or to prove B. The left tactic chooses A, and the right tactic chooses B.
zh: 证明析取 A ∨ B 的标准方式，是证明 A 或证明 B。left 策略选择 A，而 right 策略选择 B。

text: We cannot use an anonymous constructor to construct a proof of an “or” because Lean would have to guess which disjunct we are trying to prove. When we write proof terms we can use Or.inl and Or.inr instead to make the choice explicitly. Here, inl is short for “introduction left” and inr is short for “introduction right.”
html: We cannot use an anonymous constructor to construct a proof of an “or” because Lean would have to guess which disjunct we are trying to prove. When we write proof terms we can use Or.inl and Or.inr instead to make the choice explicitly. Here, inl is short for “introduction left” and inr is short for “introduction right.”
zh: 我们不能用匿名构造子来构造“或”的证明，因为 Lean 必须猜测我们要证明哪一个析取支。写证明项时，可以改用 Or.inl 和 Or.inr 来显式作出选择。这里 inl 是 “introduction left” 的缩写，inr 是 “introduction right” 的缩写。

text: It may seem strange to prove a disjunction by proving one side or the other. In practice, which case holds usually depends on a case distinction that is implicit or explicit in the assumptions and the data. The rcases tactic allows us to make use of a hypothesis of the form A ∨ B . In contrast to the use of rcases with conjunction or an existential quantifier, here the rcases tactic produces two goals. Both have the same conclusion, but in the first case, A is assumed to be true, and in the second case, B is assumed to be true. In other words, as the name suggests, the rcases tactic carries out a proof by cases. As usual, we can tell Lean what names to use for the hypotheses. In the next example, we tell Lean to use the name h on each branch.
html: It may seem strange to prove a disjunction by proving one side or the other. In practice, which case holds usually depends on a case distinction that is implicit or explicit in the assumptions and the data. The rcases tactic allows us to make use of a hypothesis of the form A ∨ B. In contrast to the use of rcases with conjunction or an existential quantifier, here the rcases tactic produces two goals. Both have the same conclusion, but in the first case, A is assumed to be true, and in the second case, B is assumed to be true. In other words, as the name suggests, the rcases tactic carries out a proof by cases. As usual, we can tell Lean what names to use for the hypotheses. In the next example, we tell Lean to use the name h on each branch.
zh: 通过证明某一边来证明析取，可能看起来有些奇怪。实践中，哪种情形成立通常取决于假设和数据中隐含或显式的情形区分。rcases 策略允许我们使用形如 A ∨ B 的假设。不同于在合取或存在量词中使用 rcases，这里 rcases 会产生两个目标。二者有相同结论，但第一种情形中假定 A 为真，第二种情形中假定 B 为真。换言之，正如其名所示，rcases 策略执行分情况证明。照常，我们可以告诉 Lean 使用什么名称来命名这些假设。在下一个例子中，我们告诉 Lean 在每个分支都使用名称 h。

text: Notice that the pattern changes from ⟨h₀, h₁⟩ in the case of a conjunction to h₀ | h₁ in the case of a disjunction. Think of the first pattern as matching against data that contains both an h₀ and a h₁ , whereas the second pattern, with the bar, matches against data that contains either an h₀ or an h₁ . In this case, because the two goals are separate, we have chosen to use the same name, h , in each case.
html: Notice that the pattern changes from ⟨h₀, h₁⟩ in the case of a conjunction to h₀ | h₁ in the case of a disjunction. Think of the first pattern as matching against data that contains both an h₀ and a h₁, whereas the second pattern, with the bar, matches against data that contains either an h₀ or an h₁. In this case, because the two goals are separate, we have chosen to use the same name, h, in each case.
zh: 注意，在合取情形中模式从 ⟨h₀, h₁⟩ 变成析取情形中的 h₀ | h₁。可以把第一个模式理解为匹配同时包含 h₀ 和 h₁ 的数据；而第二个带竖线的模式匹配包含 h₀ 或 h₁ 的数据。在这个例子中，由于两个目标彼此分离，我们选择在每种情形中使用同一名称 h。

text: The absolute value function is defined in such a way that we can immediately prove that x ≥ 0 implies |x| = x (this is the theorem abs_of_nonneg ) and x < 0 implies |x| = -x (this is abs_of_neg ). The expression le_or_gt 0 x establishes 0 ≤ x ∨ x < 0 , allowing us to split on those two cases.
html: The absolute value function is defined in such a way that we can immediately prove that x ≥ 0 implies |x| = x (this is the theorem abs_of_nonneg) and x < 0 implies |x| = -x (this is abs_of_neg). The expression le_or_gt 0 x establishes 0 ≤ x ∨ x < 0, allowing us to split on those two cases.
zh: 绝对值函数的定义方式使我们能够立即证明：x ≥ 0 蕴含 |x| = x（这是定理 abs_of_nonneg），而 x < 0 蕴含 |x| = -x（这是 abs_of_neg）。表达式 le_or_gt 0 x 建立 0 ≤ x ∨ x < 0，使我们可以按这两种情形拆分。

text: Lean also supports the computer scientists’ pattern-matching syntax for disjunction. Now the cases tactic is more attractive, because it allows us to name each case , and name the hypothesis that is introduced closer to where it is used.
html: Lean also supports the computer scientists’ pattern-matching syntax for disjunction. Now the cases tactic is more attractive, because it allows us to name each case, and name the hypothesis that is introduced closer to where it is used.
zh: Lean 也支持用于析取的计算机科学式模式匹配语法。现在 cases 策略更有吸引力，因为它允许我们命名每个 case，并在更接近使用位置的地方命名引入的假设。

text: The names inl and inr are short for “intro left” and “intro right,” respectively. Using case has the advantage that you can prove the cases in either order; Lean uses the tag to find the relevant goal. If you don’t care about that, you can use next , or match , or even a pattern-matching have .
html: The names inl and inr are short for “intro left” and “intro right,” respectively. Using case has the advantage that you can prove the cases in either order; Lean uses the tag to find the relevant goal. If you don’t care about that, you can use next, or match, or even a pattern-matching have.
zh: 名称 inl 和 inr 分别是 “intro left” 和 “intro right” 的缩写。使用 case 的优点是可以按任意顺序证明各情形；Lean 会使用标签找到相关目标。若不关心这一点，可以使用 next、match，甚至使用模式匹配 have。

text: In the case of match , we need to use the full names Or.inl and Or.inr of the canonical ways to prove a disjunction. In this textbook, we will generally use rcases to split on the cases of a disjunction.
html: In the case of match, we need to use the full names Or.inl and Or.inr of the canonical ways to prove a disjunction. In this textbook, we will generally use rcases to split on the cases of a disjunction.
zh: 在 match 的情形中，需要使用证明析取的标准方式的全名 Or.inl 和 Or.inr。本书通常会使用 rcases 来对析取进行分情况讨论。

text: Try proving the triangle inequality using the first two theorems in the next snippet. They are given the same names they have in Mathlib.
html: Try proving the triangle inequality using the first two theorems in the next snippet. They are given the same names they have in Mathlib.
zh: 请尝试使用下面代码片段中的前两个定理证明三角不等式。这些定理使用与 Mathlib 中相同的名称。

text: In case you enjoyed these (pun intended) and you want more practice with disjunction, try these.
html: In case you enjoyed these (pun intended) and you want more practice with disjunction, try these.
zh: 如果你喜欢这些例子（这里有个双关），并且想进一步练习析取，请尝试下面这些题。

text: You can also use rcases and rintro with nested disjunctions. When these result in a genuine case split with multiple goals, the patterns for each new goal are separated by a vertical bar.
html: You can also use rcases and rintro with nested disjunctions. When these result in a genuine case split with multiple goals, the patterns for each new goal are separated by a vertical bar.
zh: 也可以把 rcases 和 rintro 用于嵌套析取。当它们产生真正的多目标分情况讨论时，各个新目标的模式用竖线分隔。

text: You can still nest patterns and use the rfl keyword to substitute equations:
html: You can still nest patterns and use the rfl keyword to substitute equations:
zh: 仍然可以嵌套模式，并使用关键字 rfl 来替换等式：

text: See if you can prove the following with a single (long) line. Use rcases to unpack the hypotheses and split on cases, and use <;> linarith to solve each branch.
html: See if you can prove the following with a single (long) line. Use rcases to unpack the hypotheses and split on cases, and use <;> linarith to solve each branch.
zh: 看看你能否用一行（较长的）代码证明下面的命题。使用 rcases 拆开假设并分情况讨论，然后用 <;> linarith 解决每个分支。

text: On the real numbers, an equation x * y = 0 tells us that x = 0 or y = 0 . In Mathlib, this fact is known as eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero , and it is another nice example of how a disjunction can arise. See if you can use it to prove the following:
html: On the real numbers, an equation x * y = 0 tells us that x = 0 or y = 0. In Mathlib, this fact is known as eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero, and it is another nice example of how a disjunction can arise. See if you can use it to prove the following:
zh: 在实数上，等式 x * y = 0 告诉我们 x = 0 或 y = 0。在 Mathlib 中，这一事实名为 eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero，它也是析取如何出现的一个漂亮例子。看看你能否用它证明下面的命题：

text: Remember that you can use the ring tactic to help with calculations.
html: Remember that you can use the ring tactic to help with calculations.
zh: 记住，可以使用 ring 策略帮助完成计算。

text: In an arbitrary ring \(R\) , an element \(x\) such that \(x y = 0\) for some nonzero \(y\) is called a left zero divisor , an element \(x\) such that \(y x = 0\) for some nonzero \(y\) is called a right zero divisor , and an element that is either a left or right zero divisor is called simply a zero divisor . The theorem eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero says that the real numbers have no nontrivial zero divisors. A commutative ring with this property is called an integral domain . Your proofs of the two theorems above should work equally well in any integral domain:
html: In an arbitrary ring \(R\), an element \(x\) such that \(x y = 0\) for some nonzero \(y\) is called a left zero divisor, an element \(x\) such that \(y x = 0\) for some nonzero \(y\) is called a right zero divisor, and an element that is either a left or right zero divisor is called simply a zero divisor. The theorem eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero says that the real numbers have no nontrivial zero divisors. A commutative ring with this property is called an integral domain. Your proofs of the two theorems above should work equally well in any integral domain:
zh: 在任意环 \(R\) 中，若元素 \(x\) 满足对某个非零 \(y\) 有 \(xy = 0\)，则称 \(x\) 为左零因子；若满足对某个非零 \(y\) 有 \(yx = 0\)，则称 \(x\) 为右零因子；一个左零因子或右零因子统称为零因子。定理 eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero 表明实数没有非平凡零因子。具有这一性质的交换环称为整环。你对上面两个定理的证明，在任意整环中也同样有效：

text: In fact, if you are careful, you can prove the first theorem without using commutativity of multiplication. In that case, it suffices to assume that R is a Ring instead of an CommRing .
html: In fact, if you are careful, you can prove the first theorem without using commutativity of multiplication. In that case, it suffices to assume that R is a Ring instead of an CommRing.
zh: 事实上，如果足够小心，第一个定理不必使用乘法交换性即可证明。在这种情况下，只需假定 R 是 Ring，而不必是 CommRing。

text: Sometimes in a proof we want to split on cases depending on whether some statement is true or not. For any proposition P , we can use em P : P ∨ ¬ P . The name em is short for “excluded middle.”
html: Sometimes in a proof we want to split on cases depending on whether some statement is true or not. For any proposition P, we can use em P : P ∨ ¬ P. The name em is short for “excluded middle.”
zh: 有时在证明中，我们想根据某个命题是否为真来分情况讨论。对任意命题 P，可以使用 em P : P ∨ ¬ P。名称 em 是 “excluded middle”（排中律）的缩写。

text: Alternatively, you can use the by_cases tactic.
html: Alternatively, you can use the by_cases tactic.
zh: 另一种选择是使用 by_cases 策略。

text: Notice that the by_cases tactic lets you specify a label for the hypothesis that is introduced in each branch, in this case, h' : P in one and h' : ¬ P in the other. If you leave out the label, Lean uses h by default. Try proving the following equivalence, using by_cases to establish one direction.
html: Notice that the by_cases tactic lets you specify a label for the hypothesis that is introduced in each branch, in this case, h' : P in one and h' : ¬ P in the other. If you leave out the label, Lean uses h by default. Try proving the following equivalence, using by_cases to establish one direction.
zh: 注意，by_cases 策略允许你指定在每个分支中引入的假设标签；在这个例子中，一个分支里是 h' : P，另一个分支里是 h' : ¬ P。如果省略标签，Lean 默认使用 h。请尝试证明下面的等价命题，并用 by_cases 建立其中一个方向。

text: We now have enough skills at our disposal to do some real mathematics. In Lean, we can represent a sequence \(s_0, s_1, s_2, \ldots\) of real numbers as a function s : ℕ → ℝ . Such a sequence is said to converge to a number \(a\) if for every \(\varepsilon > 0\) there is a point beyond which the sequence remains within \(\varepsilon\) of \(a\) , that is, there is a number \(N\) such that for every \(n \ge N\) , \(| s_n - a | < \varepsilon\) . In Lean, we can render this as follows:
html: We now have enough skills at our disposal to do some real mathematics. In Lean, we can represent a sequence \(s_0, s_1, s_2, \ldots\) of real numbers as a function s : ℕ → ℝ. Such a sequence is said to converge to a number \(a\) if for every \(\varepsilon > 0\) there is a point beyond which the sequence remains within \(\varepsilon\) of \(a\), that is, there is a number \(N\) such that for every \(n \ge N\), \(| s_n - a | < \varepsilon\). In Lean, we can render this as follows:
zh: 现在我们掌握了足够多的技巧，可以做一些真正的数学。在 Lean 中，可以把实数序列 \(s_0, s_1, s_2, \ldots\) 表示为函数 s : ℕ → ℝ。若对每个 \(\varepsilon > 0\)，都存在某个位置，从那里往后序列始终位于 \(a\) 的 \(\varepsilon\) 邻域内，也就是说，存在自然数 \(N\)，使得对每个 \(n \ge N\)，都有 \(|s_n - a| < \varepsilon\)，则称该序列收敛到数 \(a\)。在 Lean 中，可以把它写成如下形式：

text: The notation ∀ ε > 0, ... is a convenient abbreviation for ∀ ε, ε > 0 → ... , and, similarly, ∀ n ≥ N, ... abbreviates ∀ n, n ≥ N → ... . And remember that ε > 0 , in turn, is defined as 0 < ε , and n ≥ N is defined as N ≤ n .
html: The notation ∀ ε > 0, ... is a convenient abbreviation for ∀ ε, ε > 0 → ..., and, similarly, ∀ n ≥ N, ... abbreviates ∀ n, n ≥ N →  .... And remember that ε > 0, in turn, is defined as 0 < ε, and n ≥ N is defined as N ≤ n.
zh: 记号 ∀ ε > 0, ... 是 ∀ ε, ε > 0 → ... 的便利缩写；类似地，∀ n ≥ N, ... 是 ∀ n, n ≥ N → ... 的缩写。还要记住，ε > 0 又定义为 0 < ε，而 n ≥ N 定义为 N ≤ n。

text: In this section, we’ll establish some properties of convergence. But first, we will discuss three tactics for working with equality that will prove useful. The first, the ext tactic, gives us a way of proving that two functions are equal. Let \(f(x) = x + 1\) and \(g(x) = 1 + x\) be functions from reals to reals. Then, of course, \(f = g\) , because they return the same value for every \(x\) . The ext tactic enables us to prove an equation between functions by proving that their values are the same at all the values of their arguments.
html: In this section, we’ll establish some properties of convergence. But first, we will discuss three tactics for working with equality that will prove useful. The first, the ext tactic, gives us a way of proving that two functions are equal. Let \(f(x) = x + 1\) and \(g(x) = 1 + x\) be functions from reals to reals. Then, of course, \(f = g\), because they return the same value for every \(x\). The ext tactic enables us to prove an equation between functions by proving that their values are the same at all the values of their arguments.
zh: 本节将建立一些关于收敛的性质。但首先，我们讨论三个处理等式时有用的策略。第一个是 ext 策略，它提供了一种证明两个函数相等的方法。设 \(f(x) = x + 1\) 和 \(g(x) = 1 + x\) 是从实数到实数的函数。那么当然有 \(f = g\)，因为它们在每个 \(x\) 处返回相同的值。ext 策略使我们能够通过证明两个函数在所有参数值处取值相同，来证明函数之间的等式。

text: We’ll see later that ext is actually more general, and also one can specify the name of the variables that appear. For instance you can try to replace ext with ext u v in the above proof. The second tactic, the congr tactic, allows us to prove an equation between two expressions by reconciling the parts that are different:
html: We’ll see later that ext is actually more general, and also one can specify the name of the variables that appear. For instance you can try to replace ext with ext u v in the above proof. The second tactic, the congr tactic, allows us to prove an equation between two expressions by reconciling the parts that are different:
zh: 稍后我们会看到，ext 实际上更加一般，并且可以指定出现的变量名。例如，你可以尝试把上面证明中的 ext 替换为 ext u v。第二个策略 congr 允许我们通过调和两个表达式中不同的部分来证明等式：

text: Here the congr tactic peels off the abs on each side, leaving us to prove a = a - b + b .
html: Here the congr tactic peels off the abs on each side, leaving us to prove a = a - b + b.
zh: 这里，congr 策略剥去两边的 abs，留下要证明的目标 a = a - b + b。

text: Finally, the convert tactic is used to apply a theorem to a goal when the conclusion of the theorem doesn’t quite match. For example, suppose we want to prove a < a * a from 1 < a . A theorem in the library, mul_lt_mul_iff_left₀ , will let us prove 1 * a < a * a . One possibility is to work backwards and rewrite the goal so that it has that form. Instead, the convert tactic lets us apply the theorem as it is, and leaves us with the task of proving the equations that are needed to make the goal match.
html: Finally, the convert tactic is used to apply a theorem to a goal when the conclusion of the theorem doesn’t quite match. For example, suppose we want to prove a < a * a from 1 < a. A theorem in the library, mul_lt_mul_iff_left₀, will let us prove 1 * a < a * a. One possibility is to work backwards and rewrite the goal so that it has that form. Instead, the convert tactic lets us apply the theorem as it is, and leaves us with the task of proving the equations that are needed to make the goal match.
zh: 最后，convert 策略用于在某个定理的结论与当前目标不完全匹配时，把该定理应用于目标。例如，假设我们想从 1 < a 证明 a < a * a。库中的一个定理 mul_lt_mul_iff_left₀ 可让我们证明 1 * a < a * a。一种做法是反向工作，把目标重写为这种形式。相反，convert 策略允许我们按原样应用该定理，并留下证明若干等式的任务，这些等式正是使目标匹配所需的。

text: This example illustrates another useful trick: when we apply an expression with an underscore and Lean can’t fill it in for us automatically, it simply leaves it for us as another goal.
html: This example illustrates another useful trick: when we apply an expression with an underscore and Lean can’t fill it in for us automatically, it simply leaves it for us as another goal.
zh: 这个例子展示了另一个有用技巧：当我们应用一个带下划线的表达式而 Lean 无法自动填入该下划线时，它会把它简单地留下来，作为另一个目标交给我们。

text: The following shows that any constant sequence \(a, a, a, \ldots\) converges.
html: The following shows that any constant sequence \(a, a, a, \ldots\) converges.
zh: 下面证明任意常值序列 \(a, a, a, \ldots\) 都收敛。

text: Lean has a tactic, simp , which can often save you the trouble of carrying out steps like rw [sub_self, abs_zero] by hand. We will tell you more about it soon.
html: Lean has a tactic, simp, which can often save you the trouble of carrying out steps like rw [sub_self, abs_zero] by hand. We will tell you more about it soon.
zh: Lean 有一个策略 simp，它常常能省去手工执行类似 rw [sub_self, abs_zero] 步骤的麻烦。我们很快会进一步介绍它。

text: For a more interesting theorem, let’s show that if s converges to a and t converges to b , then fun n ↦ s n + t n converges to a + b . It is helpful to have a clear pen-and-paper proof in mind before you start writing a formal one. Given ε greater than 0 , the idea is to use the hypotheses to obtain an Ns such that beyond that point, s is within ε / 2 of a , and an Nt such that beyond that point, t is within ε / 2 of b . Then, whenever n is greater than or equal to the maximum of Ns and Nt , the sequence fun n ↦ s n + t n should be within ε of a + b . The following example begins to implement this strategy. See if you can finish it off.
html: For a more interesting theorem, let’s show that if s converges to a and t converges to b, then fun n ↦ s n + t n converges to a + b. It is helpful to have a clear pen-and-paper proof in mind before you start writing a formal one. Given ε greater than 0, the idea is to use the hypotheses to obtain an Ns such that beyond that point, s is within ε / 2 of a, and an Nt such that beyond that point, t is within ε / 2 of b. Then, whenever n is greater than or equal to the maximum of Ns and Nt, the sequence fun n ↦ s n + t n should be within ε of a + b. The following example begins to implement this strategy. See if you can finish it off.
zh: 作为一个更有趣的定理，我们来证明：若 s 收敛到 a，且 t 收敛到 b，则 fun n ↦ s n + t n 收敛到 a + b。在开始写形式化证明之前，心中有一个清楚的纸笔证明会很有帮助。给定大于 0 的 ε，思路是使用假设得到一个 Ns，使得从该点以后，s 与 a 的距离小于 ε / 2；再得到一个 Nt，使得从该点以后，t 与 b 的距离小于 ε / 2。于是，只要 n 大于等于 Ns 和 Nt 的最大值，序列 fun n ↦ s n + t n 就应当与 a + b 的距离小于 ε。下面的例子开始实现这个策略。看看你能否把它完成。

text: As hints, you can use le_of_max_le_left and le_of_max_le_right , and norm_num can prove ε / 2 + ε / 2 = ε . Also, it is helpful to use the congr tactic to show that |s n + t n - (a + b)| is equal to |(s n - a) + (t n - b)|, since then you can use the triangle inequality. Notice that we marked all the variables s , t , a , and b implicit because they can be inferred from the hypotheses.
html: As hints, you can use le_of_max_le_left and le_of_max_le_right, and norm_num can prove ε / 2 + ε / 2 = ε. Also, it is helpful to use the congr tactic to show that |s n + t n - (a + b)| is equal to |(s n - a) + (t n - b)|, since then you can use the triangle inequality. Notice that we marked all the variables s, t, a, and b implicit because they can be inferred from the hypotheses.
zh: 作为提示，可以使用 le_of_max_le_left 和 le_of_max_le_right，而 norm_num 可以证明 ε / 2 + ε / 2 = ε。此外，使用 congr 策略证明 |s n + t n - (a + b)| 等于 |(s n - a) + (t n - b)| 也很有帮助，因为这样就可以使用三角不等式。注意，我们把所有变量 s、t、a 和 b 都标记为隐式变量，因为它们可以由假设推断出来。

text: Proving the same theorem with multiplication in place of addition is tricky. We will get there by proving some auxiliary statements first. See if you can also finish off the next proof, which shows that if s converges to a , then fun n ↦ c * s n converges to c * a . It is helpful to split into cases depending on whether c is equal to zero or not. We have taken care of the zero case, and we have left you to prove the result with the extra assumption that c is nonzero.
html: Proving the same theorem with multiplication in place of addition is tricky. We will get there by proving some auxiliary statements first. See if you can also finish off the next proof, which shows that if s converges to a, then fun n ↦ c * s n converges to c * a. It is helpful to split into cases depending on whether c is equal to zero or not. We have taken care of the zero case, and we have left you to prove the result with the extra assumption that c is nonzero.
zh: 把同一个定理中的加法替换为乘法后，证明会更棘手。我们将先证明一些辅助命题。请看看能否也完成下一个证明，它表明若 s 收敛到 a，则 fun n ↦ c * s n 收敛到 c * a。按 c 是否等于零分情况讨论会很有帮助。我们已经处理了零的情形，并留下了带有额外假设 c 非零时的结果让你证明。

text: The next theorem is also independently interesting: it shows that a convergent sequence is eventually bounded in absolute value. We have started you off; see if you can finish it.
html: The next theorem is also independently interesting: it shows that a convergent sequence is eventually bounded in absolute value. We have started you off; see if you can finish it.
zh: 下一个定理本身也很有意思：它表明收敛序列最终在绝对值意义下有界。我们已经给出开头；看看你能否完成它。

text: In fact, the theorem could be strengthened to assert that there is a bound b that holds for all values of n . But this version is strong enough for our purposes, and we will see at the end of this section that it holds more generally.
html: In fact, the theorem could be strengthened to assert that there is a bound b that holds for all values of n. But this version is strong enough for our purposes, and we will see at the end of this section that it holds more generally.
zh: 事实上，这个定理还可以加强为：存在一个界 b，对所有 n 都成立。但当前版本已足以满足我们的需要，并且我们将在本节末尾看到它在更一般情形下也成立。

text: The next lemma is auxiliary: we prove that if s converges to a and t converges to 0 , then fun n ↦ s n * t n converges to 0 . To do so, we use the previous theorem to find a B that bounds s beyond some point N₀ . See if you can understand the strategy we have outlined and finish the proof.
html: The next lemma is auxiliary: we prove that if s converges to a and t converges to 0, then fun n ↦ s n * t n converges to 0. To do so, we use the previous theorem to find a B that bounds s beyond some point N₀. See if you can understand the strategy we have outlined and finish the proof.
zh: 下一个引理是辅助性的：我们证明若 s 收敛到 a，且 t 收敛到 0，则 fun n ↦ s n * t n 收敛到 0。为此，我们使用前一个定理找到一个 B，它在某个点 N₀ 之后界住 s。看看你能否理解我们勾勒出的策略并完成证明。

text: If you have made it this far, congratulations! We are now within striking distance of our theorem. The following proof finishes it off.
html: If you have made it this far, congratulations! We are now within striking distance of our theorem. The following proof finishes it off.
zh: 如果你已经做到这里，恭喜！我们距离目标定理已经很近了。下面的证明将完成它。

text: For another challenging exercise, try filling out the following sketch of a proof that limits are unique. (If you are feeling bold, you can delete the proof sketch and try proving it from scratch.)
html: For another challenging exercise, try filling out the following sketch of a proof that limits are unique. (If you are feeling bold, you can delete the proof sketch and try proving it from scratch.)
zh: 作为另一个有挑战性的练习，请尝试补全下面的证明草稿，证明极限唯一。（如果你很有信心，可以删除证明草稿并尝试从零开始证明。）

text: We close the section with the observation that our proofs can be generalized. For example, the only properties that we have used of the natural numbers is that their structure carries a partial order with min and max . You can check that everything still works if you replace ℕ everywhere by any linear order α :
html: We close the section with the observation that our proofs can be generalized. For example, the only properties that we have used of the natural numbers is that their structure carries a partial order with min and max. You can check that everything still works if you replace ℕ everywhere by any linear order α:
zh: 本节最后指出，我们的证明可以推广。例如，我们对自然数所使用的唯一性质，是其结构带有偏序以及 min 和 max。可以检查，如果把各处的 ℕ 替换为任意线性序 α，一切仍然成立：

text: In Section 11.1 , we will see that Mathlib has mechanisms for dealing with convergence in vastly more general terms, not only abstracting away particular features of the domain and codomain, but also abstracting over different types of convergence.
html: In Section 11.1, we will see that Mathlib has mechanisms for dealing with convergence in vastly more general terms, not only abstracting away particular features of the domain and codomain, but also abstracting over different types of convergence.
zh: 在 第 11.1 节 中，我们会看到 Mathlib 有机制以远为一般的方式处理收敛；它不仅抽象掉定义域和陪域的具体特征，也抽象不同类型的收敛。
